message:
perf(session): optimize entry ID generation with fast-path linear scan

Avoid constructing a full HashSet of all existing entry IDs on every
call to next_entry_id(). For sessions with thousands of entries, the
HashSet construction was O(n) work repeated on every message append.

The new fast path generates a UUID v4 candidate and does a quick linear
scan via entry_id_exists() to check for collisions. Since UUIDs have
negligible collision probability, this loop almost always exits on the
first iteration. The loop retries up to 100 times before falling back
to the original HashSet-based generate_entry_id() as a safety net.

Add entry_id_exists() helper that short-circuits on the first match
using Iterator::any(), avoiding the allocation overhead of collecting
all IDs into a set when only an existence check is needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
